narrative:
Lee Kang-in, representing South Korea, operates as an Attacking Midfield. His overall rating of 7.18/10 places him among the top 9% of attacking midfields at this tournament. This rating reflects both his club form over the last two seasons and his performances at this World Cup.

His creative impact is clear, evidenced by 0.333 assists per 90 minutes and 2.333 key passes per 90 minutes at this World Cup. However, his direct goal threat is limited, with an expected goals (xG) per 90 minutes of just 0.046 and only 1.0 shots per 90 minutes in the tournament. These figures suggest he is more of a creator than a finisher.

Lee Kang-in serves as a vital creative hub for South Korea, orchestrating attacks from the midfield. Scouts and fans should watch for his continued ability to unlock defenses with incisive passing. Developing a more direct goal threat could elevate his overall impact in future matches.
